# Pr 95312 Unify Handshake Consumer Ux And Retry Bump

Source type: github
Document ID: dsid_88b39d13bd584ef8b1422d76994808c7
Release: v1.0.0
Benchmark: EnterpriseRAG-Bench synthetic upstream data
Cross-SDK: unify streaming handshake, consumer ergonomics, and auth/retry semantics (Python/TS/Go)

Motivation: We have diverging streaming ergonomics across Python/TS/Go SDKs and subtle auth-retry edge cases that manifest as half-open streams or token race conditions during refresh. This PR unifies three areas: 1) a minimal, consistent 3-way auth handshake for stream starts and reconnects; 2) a small, opinionated consumer surface that makes backpressure and heartbeats explicit but ergonomic; 3) a single, test-backed retry policy and token-refresh hook that avoids duplicate requests during auth rotation.

Summary of changes (high level):
- Introduce a lightweight stream handshake: CLIENT_HELLO -> AUTH_OK -> SERVER_READY. Implemented in each SDK with shared semantics and compatibility fallbacks for older servers.
- Consumer ergonomics: new thin abstraction that replaces ad-hoc iterators/callbacks. Python exposes StreamConsumer (AsyncIterator + context manager) with buffer_size and heartbeat_ack semantics; TS exposes createStreamConsumer({onMessage, onComplete, bufferSize}) and an async iterable wrapper; Go exposes StreamReader(ctx) that implements io.Reader-like semantics for single-message reads and a Cancel() helper.
- Auth/retries: unified RetryWithAuthRefresh policy across SDKs. Retries are jittered exponential with token refresh hook invoked once per logical request; transparent idempotency guard added for non-idempotent unary calls.
- Backwards compatibility: consumers detect old handshake flows and fall back to legacy behavior for servers that do not return AUTH_OK. Migration warnings are surfaced at runtime (single warn log per process).
- Tests: added unit tests for handshake transitions and integration e2e stream tests in a local harness that simulates mid-stream token expiration.
- Version bumps: python 0.12.0, typescript 1.9.0, go 0.8.3.

Why this shape: the platform-level serving runtime already relies on explicit stream lifecycle events; exposing a small, consistent surface across SDKs reduces footguns (double-sends during reauth, leaked goroutines or pending async generators) and improves observability for consumers.

Migration notes:
- Python: replace raw async for msg in client.stream() with: async with client.stream_consumer() as c: async for msg in c: ...; short-lived code paths still supported but deprecated.
- TS: prefer createStreamConsumer over stream().iterator() when you need backpressure controls.
- Go: use NewStreamReader(ctx) to ensure reads cancel cleanly on token refresh.

Checklist: 
- [x] Cross-SDK API proposals agreed with SDK leads (Raj/Elena)
- [x] Unit + integration tests added
- [x] CI flake triaged and rerun (see CI summary)
- [x] Changelog & migration guide updated

Notes: this PR intentionally keeps signal surface minimal (3 flags + 2 callbacks) to avoid burdening consumers. See linked Linear ENG-2142 for design doc.
Raj: Can we avoid a second token refresh race if two concurrent streams call the hook? Suggest ensuring the hook is single-flight per token_id. Response: implemented singleflight guard in shared retry_policy with metrics (auth_refresh_locks).
Elena: TS consumer ergonomics look good but question on buffer default. Response: default buffer_size changed to 16 and documented rationale (latency vs memory).
Samir: Go Cancel() semantics — will it leak goroutines when Cancel is called mid-read? Response: added explicit test and closed goroutine via consistent context cancellation path; verified in go race run.
CI-bot: e2e streaming failed when simulating token expiry. Author: added extra wait/retry in harness and hardened backoff; reran and passed.
- SDKs: unify streaming handshake semantics across Python/TS/Go; introduce ergonomic StreamConsumer/StreamReader abstractions.
- Auth: single-flight token refresh hook and unified RetryWithAuthRefresh policy to avoid duplicate in-flight retries.
- Backwards compatibility: servers without AUTH_OK are supported with harmless warnings.
- Version bumps: python 0.12.0, typescript 1.9.0, go 0.8.3.
- Migration guidance added to docs/migration/streaming-migration.md
